flag.detail: section-4 acceptance assertion S4.A4 requires 'A zip of the .sdPlugin directory is present alongside' (and the parent acceptance text restates 'A zip of the .sdPlugin directory is present alongside'). No *.zip file exists under output/integration/ or output/builders/packaging/. The integration manifest acknowledges this explicitly (zip_distribution.status = 'not_produced_by_text_only_build'). S4.A4 verifier is critic_final_sweep, so this is the right place to surface the violation. Coordinator nevertheless marked section-4 status: verified. Source: td_plan, covers section-4.acceptance — this is an internal-consistency check (the plan's own packaging contract is unsatisfied), not a Principle H external-system concern. Note: section-4 acceptance also asserts 'output/final/ contains exactly one *.sdPlugin directory'; output/final/ is empty at final-sweep time because Orchestrator copies after CV. That part of the assertion is properly deferred to post-CV delivery and is not flagged here.
flag.scope_recommendation: Route to Arbiter. Either (a) Sev 0 trivial-fix if Coordinator can produce a zip (text-only sandbox cannot reliably; likely not eligible), (b) amend section-4 acceptance to make zip optional with documented alternative, or (c) carry into Uncertainty Manifest as a known artifact gap. The Uncertainty Manifest already includes a related ws-bundling note but does not explicitly enumerate the missing zip.

flag.detail: Per file_schemas.md § Coverage-Required Fields, decisions/discovery/ledger-v1.json#inflection_points[].default_branch is coverage-required (one row per IP). The assertion set covers IP5.default_branch and IP6.default_branch directly, but for IP1..IP4 the only assertions covering the locked decision point at the TD layer (sections-v1.json#inflection_resolutions[ip_id=IPn].chosen_branch). The spirit of coverage IS satisfied — IPn.chosen_branch is the resolution of IPn.default_branch and is itself coverage-required (and covered) — but the strict property check (every coverage-required field has at least one assertion with `covers` pointing to that exact field) fails for IP1.default_branch, IP2.default_branch, IP3.default_branch, IP4.default_branch. Note also two stylistic-but-real anomalies: DCA.FC.3 has covers pointing to '#first_contact_requirements[id=FC.3]' (no .description suffix), one minor field-suffix mismatch from the coverage-required canonical 'first_contact_requirements[].description'; all other FC DCAs use the .description suffix.
flag.schema_amendment_candidate: v1.10: clarify whether default_branch coverage is satisfied transitively by chosen_branch coverage, or require DCAs that point explicitly at ledger#inflection_points[id=IPn].default_branch alongside chosen_branch DCAs.

flag.detail: history/log.jsonl exists but is zero bytes. Per file_schemas.md, Historian writes append-only entries. Coordinator under inline dispatch produced build-summary.md and decision-index.json (the v1.6-required delivery artifacts) but did not append any structured log entries to log.jsonl. Required for delivery per v1.6 = build-summary.md + decision-index.json, both present; log.jsonl is not explicitly listed as a delivery requirement in the table. Flagging as low because the file exists and the delivery-required artifacts are present, but the absence of any log entries under inline dispatch is an architecture-vs-implementation gap worth noting for v1.10.

Editor's review-v1.json verdict_rationale invokes the rationale 'The architecture's North Star (always deliver; surface honesty in the run-report; do not halt to ask the user) makes route_to_user out of scope; the build proceeds and the user receives a transparent record.' The phrase 'North Star contract overrides the gate' is referenced in file_schemas.md's loop-semantics note for Editor (iteration cap = 3, after which the build proceeds carrying unresolved findings as Uncertainty Manifest entries). However, applying this rationale to a first-pass verdict (no prior amendments, no iteration-cap exhaustion) to justify pass_with_recommendations instead of route_to_user is an Editor-introduced framing. The verdict itself is defensible on Principle E/F/H grounds (all six structural checks pass, no Principle violations, only external_source_unreachable surfacings) and the verdict does not change correctness. Flagging as audit_meta: editor_improvisation severity LOW; v1.10 amendment candidate to either (a) explicitly codify that 'North Star overrides the gate' applies to first-pass verdicts as well, or (b) require Editor to use a different rationale when the iteration cap has not been exhausted.

The integrated artifact at output/integration/com.autobuilder.applemusic-now-playing.sdPlugin/ has require('ws') in js/host.js and declares 'ws':'^8.16.0' in package.json, but no node_modules/ directory is bundled and the README does not instruct the end user to run npm install. On a clean install via the documented '%APPDATA%\HotSpot\StreamDock\plugins' copy path, the host's Node.js 20 runtime will throw 'Cannot find module ws' at startup. The integration manifest acknowledges this in missing_or_known_gaps and the build-complete Uncertainty Manifest includes 'node_modules/ws not bundled; install or host-provision required.' No acceptance_assertion explicitly requires the artifact to be standalone-installable without an npm step. The closest implicated assertion is FC.1 ('without requiring the user to use developer tooling, recompile, or hand-edit configuration files') — running 'npm install' inside the plugin directory IS developer tooling. FC.1's verifier is prompt_named_verb (subsumed by PNV.1), which is already documented as unverifiable_without_hardware. Per the briefing's honest-assessment guidance: 'If not [explicitly required by an acceptance_assertion], it's a CV/Sev 4 surfacing concern.' Flagging as low for Critic; routing to CV's principle_h_skips / Uncertainty Manifest is the appropriate path. Note that the README's reference to 'extract it first; the zip distribution' compounds the issue because no zip exists either (see separate flag on S4.A4).
